title: SonicWall Connection Table Saturation and Max-Session Pressure (SonicWall)
dataSources: `sourcetype=dell:sonicwall` resource alerts or SNMP-derived connection counts
spl:
index=netops sourcetype="dell:sonicwall" earliest=-4h
| where match(_raw,"(?i)connection.*table|max connection|session.*limit|resource.*exhaust")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<curr_conn>\d+)\s*(active )?connections"
| timechart span=5m max(curr_conn) by host
description: State table exhaustion causes random TCP failures during DNS storms or SYN floods even if bandwidth is fine.
value: Triggers timely firmware tuning, connection limit adjustments, or DDoS escalation.
implementation: Ingest SNMP `currConn` or syslog threshold traps. Compare to licensed maximum.

references.title: Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk
Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall reference

grandmaExplanation: State table exhaustion causes random TCP failures during DNS storms or SYN floods even if bandwidth is fine — we monitor SonicWall logs so teams can act before users feel pain.
